---
title: "Getting Started with vcf2dosage"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Getting Started with vcf2dosage}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

## Overview

vcf2dosage converts VCF files into numeric dosage matrices ready for
genomic selection (GS) and genome-wide association studies (GWAS). It
handles diploid and polyploid species with automatic ploidy detection.

## Basic Usage

```{r basic}
library(vcf2dosage)

# One function does everything
result <- vcf2dosage("my_genotypes.vcf.gz")
# Step 1: Reading VCF
# > Read 15,234 variants x 384 samples
# Step 2: Extracting genotype calls
# Step 3: Detecting ploidy
# > Detected ploidy: 2
# Step 4: Converting to dosage
# Step 5: Filtering
# > MAF filter (>= 0.05): removed 1,847/15,234 variants
# > Missingness filter (<= 20%): removed 312/15,234 variants
# Done
# > Final: 13,075 variants x 384 samples (ploidy = 2)

# The result contains everything you need
dim(result$dosage) # variants x samples
dim(result$dosage_t) # samples x markers (for GS)
head(result$map) # marker positions
```

## Polyploid Species

```{r polyploid}
# Tetraploid potato
result <- vcf2dosage("potato.vcf.gz", ploidy = 4)
# Dosage values: 0, 1, 2, 3, or 4

# Hexaploid wheat
result <- vcf2dosage("wheat.vcf.gz", ploidy = 6)
# Dosage values: 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6

# Auto-detect ploidy
result <- vcf2dosage("unknown.vcf.gz")
result$ploidy
```

## Custom Filtering

```{r filtering}
result <- vcf2dosage(
  "genotypes.vcf.gz",
  min_maf = 0.01, # Keep rarer variants
  max_missing_variant = 0.1, # Stricter: max 10% missing
  max_missing_sample = 0.3 # Remove samples with >30% missing
)

# Check what was removed
result$filter_summary
```

## Export for GS Packages

```{r export}
# rrBLUP: centered dosage matrix
Z <- export_rrblup(result$dosage)
# library(rrBLUP)
# fit <- mixed.solve(y = pheno$yield, Z = Z)

# GWASpoly: marker info + dosage
geno <- export_gwaspoly(result$dosage, result$vcf$fix)
# library(GWASpoly)
# data <- read.GWASpoly(pheno.file, geno.file)

# BGLR: imputed matrix for Bayesian models
X <- export_bglr(result$dosage, impute = TRUE)
# library(BGLR)
# fit <- BGLR(y = pheno$yield, ETA = list(MRK = list(X = X, model = "BRR")))

# sommer: genomic relationship matrix
G <- export_sommer(result$dosage, as_relationship = TRUE)
# library(sommer)
# fit <- mmer(yield ~ 1, random = ~vsr(genotype, Gu = G), data = pheno)
```

## Step-by-Step Usage

For more control, use the individual functions:

```{r stepwise}
# 1. Read VCF
vcf <- read_vcf("genotypes.vcf.gz")

# 2. Extract GT field
gt <- extract_gt(vcf)

# 3. Check ploidy
ploidy <- detect_ploidy(gt)
table(ploidy)

# 4. Convert to dosage
dosage <- gt_to_dosage(gt)

# 5. Filter
dosage <- filter_maf(dosage, maf = 0.05)$dosage
dosage <- filter_missingness(dosage, max_missing = 0.2)$dosage

# 6. Quality summary
summary <- variant_summary(dosage, vcf$fix)
```
